summary: Tested dual Whisper transcription approach for pronunciation feedback. All 15 backend tests PASSED. Verified: charitable_transcription parameter in tool schema + subagent + executor, voice.py dual-pass logic, pronunciation_hint construction, [PRONUNCIATION CONTEXT] tag in prompts, and POST /api/conversations endpoint.

tests: check_pronunciation tool schema has charitable_transcription parameter with description
run_pronunciation_feedback_subagent accepts charitable_transcription with None default
tool_executor extracts and passes charitable_transcription to subagent
voice.py has transcribe calls with language parameter for charitable pass
voice.py builds pronunciation_hint when transcriptions differ
pronunciation_hint appended to history_for_agent (not saved to DB)
Tutor system prompts mention [PRONUNCIATION CONTEXT] tag
Tutor system prompts explain transcription-based pronunciation detection
POST /api/conversations creates conversation with correct native_language, target_language, message_count
Subagent uses charitable_transcription in its context/logic
Subagent has compare_phrases and break_down_words tools

critical_code_review_comments: Dual Whisper approach correctly implemented: Pass 1 (auto-detect) captures literal, Pass 2 (target language hint) captures charitable interpretation
pronunciation_hint correctly appended only to agent history (not saved to DB) - good design to avoid persisting ephemeral context
System prompts properly instruct agent on when to use check_pronunciation tool based on [PRONUNCIATION CONTEXT] tag presence
charitable_transcription parameter properly flows: tool schema -> tool_executor -> subagent
Subagent intelligently uses charitable_transcription to identify specific mispronounced words
seed_data_creation: Test user test@pronunciation.com already existed from previous iteration
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dual Whisper pronunciation approach fully verified. Key implementation points: 1) voice.py lines 67-85 do dual transcription (auto-detect then target-lang hint), 2) lines 101-109 build pronunciation_hint with [PRONUNCIATION CONTEXT] tag when transcriptions differ, 3) line 132 appends hint to agent history only (not DB), 4) check_pronunciation tool has charitable_transcription parameter, 5) subagent uses charitable vs literal comparison to identify mispronunciations. No actual audio testing possible - only code structure verification.
